what_it_does: A single-file CSS stylesheet for the canvas application. It maps legacy CSS custom properties to a corpus design-system token set, defines global theming (a dark warm-gold 'commander's bridge' atmosphere with aurora, grain, and breathing motion), makes the tldraw board transparent so the atmosphere shows through, and lays out a large family of overlay HUD regions: toolbar, palette, inspector panel, activity feed, right-hand-man chat, walkthrough/review card, workshop, settings modal, review/studio shells, builder/forager/greeting surfaces, and a lattice projection view. It also provides responsive adaptations for tablet and phone, including a bottom tabbar, bottom sheets, and a recent mobile command-model rethink.
observations: The :root block aliases legacy variable names (--panel, --ink, --sig, etc.) to corpus tokens and explicitly avoids redeclaring colliding names like --bg and --line.
The global theme uses a warm-gold aurora via body::before, a fixed noise-grain film via body::after, and a transparent tldraw background.
Entrance stagger animations (.as-top, .as-rail, .as-panel, .as-canvas .activity/.rhm) make chrome regions rise in on load.
A shared 'kit' vocabulary (.kit-sechead, .kit-lanehead, .kit-badge, .kit-surface, .kit-empty) is used across regions.
Node cards (.node-card) carry status-driven borders and a pulsing 'idle' animation while body.canvas-running is active.
Port nubs (.port-nub) hang off node edges for drag-to-wire connections.
The inspector, Grow, Inbox, Activity, History, Versions, SelfChanges, AddressHelp, Fleet, and other right-rail regions each have layout-only CSS that composes the kit primitives.
A full-viewport settings modal (.settings-scrim/.settings-panel) uses a section rail and scrollable content area.
A review/studio workspace (.review-shell, .studio-shell) provides a three-column gallery/stage/RHM layout with token slots for Claude Design to override.
Mobile rules at 1099/820/699px restructure the app shell, hide or sheet-ify overlays, add a bottom tabbar, and implement a 'one bar + long-press tray' command model.
The lattice view (.lattice-wrap) is a full-screen canvas overlay with its own foot controls, scrub/zoom/rung sliders, and info cards.
Comments throughout label blocks with feature IDs and dates (F1, WAVE-2, L10, A2, C1, etc.) and note deletions of legacy rules.
Several rules use !important and source-order specificity notes to win over legacy or kit borders (e.g., .ui-spotlight, .ui-indicated).
standouts: The file is a monolithic stylesheet that acts as theme layer, component library, layout system, and responsive/mobile behavior all at once.
The 'living instrument' atmosphere — animated warm-gold aurora, fractal-noise grain, breathing presence dot — is a central aesthetic signal.
The recent mobile command-model section is a total rethink: one top bar, bottom tabbar sheets, and a long-press-to-point tray replacing many desktop overlays.
The lattice projection view introduces a distinct spatial/temporal UI with time-radius, semantic zoom, rungs, and nucleation readouts.
conventions: CSS custom properties are aliased to a central corpus (design-system.css / tokens.json) rather than declared with literal values.
Class names are mostly flat and region-prefixed (e.g., .rhm-, .ibx-, .bi-, .br-, .cog-, .ahelp-).
Comments tag blocks with feature IDs, dates, and rationale, often noting what legacy rules were deleted.
Colour is supposed to come from tokens only; color-mix is used when no exact token exists.
Responsive breakpoints are set at 1099px, 820px, 699px, and 980px.
concerns: The file is very long and mixes global theming, component styling, layout, animation, responsive behavior, and experimental views, creating a high-maintenance surface.
Many properties depend on CSS variables defined in external files with no fallback values, so missing tokens would silently break rendering.
Specificity battles and !important usage (spotlight, indication, mobile overrides) suggest fragility against future source-order changes.
Some hardcoded values remain despite the token-only aspiration (px widths, font stack 'Fraunces', z-index numbers, SVG geometry).
Mobile rules hide or reposition many desktop surfaces; this risks duplicated logic and hidden accessibility/keyboard issues.
Comments contain 'needs-tim' and 'FORM' markers indicating unfinished design decisions.
notes: This CSS is the visual contract of the canvas app: it aliases legacy tokens to a corpus design system, paints a warm-gold living-instrument atmosphere, and lays out a large family of HUD overlays with a recent mobile command-model rethink.
questions: Which component toggles body.canvas-running and the data-mtab attribute used by the responsive rules?
Where are the corpus tokens (--acc, --sig, --s1, --kind-content, etc.) defined, and what are their actual values?
Are the feature IDs (F1, U2, C1, L10, A2, etc.) tracked in an external spec or issue tracker?
Is the lattice view a separate route or an overlay within the same app shell?
How are JavaScript-driven animations and gestures made to respect the prefers-reduced-motion preference?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be a neutral, token-driven look layer for the canvas app; the file does that, but it also accumulates layout, responsive behavior, animation, and component-like rules, making it more of a combined theme+component+layout stylesheet than a pure look layer.
apparent_themes: Living instrument / warm-gold commander's bridge aesthetic.
Recognition by sight: colour and tone carry state meaning.
Reflects-never-owns: CSS presents, logic lives elsewhere.
Fail-loud legibility: errors and unknown states are visibly distinct.
Mobile-first rethink for phone canvas interaction.
intention_signals: The file is trying to unify a sprawling canvas application under one visual language, migrate legacy styles into a shared kit, and make the interface feel alive and responsive across desktop, tablet, and phone without rebuilding the underlying tldraw board.
novelty: The combination of token-aliased global atmosphere (aurora/grain), a kit-based component vocabulary, and a recent mobile 'one bar + long-press tray' command model in one stylesheet is unusual; the lattice projection view with time/scrub/zoom/rung controls is also a distinct spatial UI.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, file-local styling contract that maps legacy variables to corpus tokens, defines the app's atmosphere and motion, and provides layout and responsive rules for a large set of HUD regions; a fused system would keep the token-aliasing root, the kit primitive vocabulary, and the mobile shell adaptations as a candidate to fold in.
summary_for_embedding: A monolithic CSS stylesheet for a tldraw-based canvas app that aliases legacy variables to a corpus design token set, implements a warm-gold living-instrument theme with animated atmosphere, and lays out responsive HUD overlays including toolbar, panels, chat, walkthrough, settings, review/studio views, and a lattice projection UI.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the single visual and layout contract for the canvas application, unifying legacy and new UI under a token-driven design system while supporting desktop, tablet, and a rethought mobile command interface.
